title: Redis
description: Redis: data structures, caching patterns, persistence, expiration and high availability.

questions.question: What is Redis and why is it so fast? Name its core data structures (string, hash, list, set, sorted set) and give a practical use case for each.
Describe common caching patterns with Redis: cache-aside, read-through and write-through/write-behind. Which one is most common in practice and what consistency problems does it have?
How does key expiration and eviction work in Redis? Explain TTL, the difference between eviction policies like noeviction, allkeys-lru and volatile-lru, and when you would choose each.
Compare Redis persistence options: RDB snapshots vs AOF. What are the trade-offs in durability, recovery time and performance, and can you combine them?

How do you scale Redis for high availability? Compare replication with Sentinel against Redis Cluster, and explain what problems distributed locks (e.g. SETNX / Redlock) try to solve and why they are tricky.
